title: IAM, Bucket Policies, Access Points, and Tenant Isolation · Contract Defense
summary: A production board compares near-miss TypeScript and AWS decisions for iam, bucket policies, access points, and tenant isolation and accepts only runtime validation, bounded ownership, causal changed-case evidence, and an explicit controlled AWS transfer gate. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for least-privilege TypeScript authorization proof. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, evaluate identity and resource policies by principal, action, exact resource, condition, session, explicit deny, and organization boundary.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, enforce Block Public Access and bucket-owner-enforced Object Ownership while explaining what each does and does not authorize.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, derive tenant-safe keys and list prefixes server-side and bind allowed actions and conditions without trusting user-supplied policy fragments.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Principal Action Resource Condition · changed case · Quiz Principal…
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 6884 during a AWS SDK v3 dependency canary. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to evaluate a runtime-validated explicit-deny-aware tenant authorization record; keep every external value unknown until a runtime validator accepts it; then replace one typed fixture with unknown runtime input and prove rejection before AWS use. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, evaluate identity and resource policies by principal, action, exact resource, condition, session, explicit deny, and organization boundary.

Which action demonstrates Principal Action Resource Condition without dropping earlier contracts?
